data.title: Macron Calls for Calm Ahead of Far-Right March in Lyon
data.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has called for calm in anticipation of a march in Lyon organized by far-right groups. Bloomberg posted on X that the march is intended to honor a man who was beaten to death in a street fight. Macron's appeal comes amid concerns about potential unrest during the event. Authorities are on high alert to ensure public safety and prevent any escalation of violence. The situation remains tense as the march approaches, with officials urging participants to maintain peace.

data.title: Ethereum Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: Ethereum's price fluctuations may lead to substantial liquidation events on major centralized exchanges. According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass indicates that if Ethereum's price falls below $1,874, the cumulative liquidation intensity for long positions across mainstream centralized exchanges could reach $860 million. Conversely, if Ethereum surpasses $2,057, the cumulative liquidation intensity for short positions could amount to $732 million.

data.title: Shift in Commodity Markets: From Free Trade to Barriers and Hoarding
data.description:
The global commodity markets are experiencing a significant transformation, moving away from the era of free trade towards a landscape characterized by barriers and hoarding. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the changing dynamics in these markets. This shift is driven by various geopolitical and economic factors, leading countries to prioritize their own resources and implement protective measures.

The trend of hoarding and imposing trade barriers is becoming increasingly prevalent as nations seek to secure their supply chains and mitigate risks associated with global uncertainties. This approach contrasts sharply with the previous emphasis on open markets and unrestricted trade, which facilitated the flow of commodities across borders.

Experts suggest that this change could have profound implications for global trade, potentially leading to increased volatility and price fluctuations in commodity markets. As countries focus on self-sufficiency, the traditional principles of free trade are being challenged, prompting a reevaluation of international trade policies.

The impact of these developments is being felt across various sectors, including agriculture, energy, and metals, as nations adapt to the new realities of commodity trading. The shift towards protectionism and resource hoarding underscores the need for strategic planning and collaboration to navigate the evolving landscape of global trade.

data.title: IoTeX: We have noticed suspicious activity in the token vault and are tracking and freezing the hacker's assets.
data.description: IoTeX issued a statement on the X platform stating that it has taken note of recent reports of suspicious activity regarding the IoTeX token vault. The team is assessing and controlling the situation. Preliminary estimates indicate that the potential losses are far lower than the rumored amount. IoTeX has coordinated with major exchanges and security partners to actively assist in tracing and freezing the hacker's assets. The situation is currently under control, and IoTeX will continue to monitor the situation and update the community on its progress.

data.title: Ark Invest Increases Stakes in Bitmine and Bullish Amid Crypto Dip
data.description:
Ark Invest increased positions in Bitmine and Bullish, reinforcing crypto exposure amid market dips and broad declines in US equities.
source: https://catenaa.com/markets/cryptocurrencies/ark-invest-increases-stakes-in-bitmine-and-bullish-amid-crypto-dip/

data.title: STOCKS | Duquesne Family Office Adjusts Portfolio with Major Moves
data.description:
Stanley Druckenmiller's Duquesne Family Office has made significant changes to its investment portfolio. According to NS3.AI, the fund has notably increased its holdings in tech giants Alphabet and Amazon. In contrast, it has completely exited positions in financial institutions such as Citigroup, Bank of America, and Capital One.

Additionally, the fund has reduced its stakes in Meta Platforms, Dick’s Sporting Goods, and Texas Roadhouse. New investments have been initiated in Delta Air Lines, Goldman Sachs, and Zillow. The total value of the fund is nearing $4.5 billion.

data.title: Brazil and India Forge Agreement on Critical Minerals
data.description: Brazil and India have entered into a framework agreement to collaborate on the processing of critical minerals. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this partnership aims to ensure a stable supply of rare earth elements amid global disruptions. The agreement signifies a strategic move by both nations to secure essential resources necessary for various industries. This collaboration is expected to enhance the processing capabilities and supply chain resilience for these vital minerals.

data.title: Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller Pours $290,836,000 Into Two Assets, Exits Exposure To Three Major US Banks
data.description:
Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller is boosting his stake in a pair of Magnificent 7 stocks while eliminating exposure to the banking industry. New filings show Duquesne Family Office bolstered its holdings in Alphabet (GOOGL) by 277% to 385,000 shares and its holdings in Amazon (AMZN) by 69% to 737,940 shares.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2026/02/21/billionaire-stanley-druckenmiller-pours-290836000-into-two-assets-exits-exposure-to-three-major-us-banks/

data.title: Trump’s World Liberty Forum Blends Crypto, Wall Street, and Politics
data.description:
President Donald Trump’s family-owned crypto venture, World Liberty Financial (WLF), hosted its inaugural World Liberty Forum at Mar-a-Lago in Palm Beach, Florida, bringing together financial executives, regulators, celebrities, and political allies under one roof.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/trumps-world-liberty-forum-blends-crypto-wall-street-and-politics/

data.title: Thailand to Continue Trade Talks with U.S. Following Supreme Court Ruling
data.description: Thailand's Commerce Minister Suphajee Suthumpun announced that the country will proceed with trade negotiations with the United States after a decision by the U.S. Supreme Court. According to Jin10, the ruling is expected to influence the dynamics of international trade discussions between the two nations. The minister emphasized Thailand's commitment to strengthening economic ties and exploring new opportunities for collaboration with the U.S. The decision comes amid ongoing efforts to enhance bilateral trade relations and address mutual interests in various sectors.

data.title: Japan's Yen Hits 53-Year Low in Real Effective Exchange Rate
data.description: Recently, Japan's yen has reached its lowest level in 53 years in terms of real effective exchange rate, reflecting the country's prolonged economic stagnation. According to Jin10, the latest data from the Bank for International Settlements shows that in January, the yen's real effective exchange rate index fell to 67.73, the lowest since Japan adopted a floating exchange rate system in 1973. This index is a crucial measure of a currency's overall purchasing power and international competitiveness. Analysts indicate that the yen's weakening purchasing power highlights the structural issues Japan's economy has faced over the long term. The country's economic growth remains sluggish, and persistently low interest rates continue to exert pressure on the yen's real effective exchange rate.

data.title: AI Developments Poised to Transform Industries, Says Citic Securities
data.description:
On February 21, Citic Securities released a report highlighting the potential impact of advancements in AI technologies on various industries. According to Jin10, the report emphasizes the evolution of native multimodal and world model technologies, which are expected to reshape sectors such as marketing, film, and gaming.

Citic Securities notes that Anthropic's release of Claude Opus 4.6, featuring Agent Teams and adaptive thinking capabilities, has significantly integrated with the Office ecosystem and facilitated complex engineering task management. This development is driving deeper AI penetration in verticals like finance and law.

Meanwhile, OpenAI has launched GPT-5.3-Codex, which not only sets new standards in programming and terminal operations but also demonstrates AI's ability to autonomously develop through environmental control and self-construction.

In the multimodal domain, ByteDance's Seedance 2.0 has begun internal testing, addressing video generation consistency issues through comprehensive multimodal referencing and precise lens control. This initiative is expected to collaborate with Doubao and Seedream to form a complete multimodal matrix, significantly reducing content production costs and accelerating commercialization.

data.title: A wallet suspected of being linked to Infini Hacker exchanged 3003 ETH for 87 WBTC.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a wallet suspected to be associated with Infini Hacker (0x4b61...beb6) exchanged 3,003 ETH for 87 WBTC at a price of $67,700, worth $5.89 million, and then sent it to another wallet.

data.title: Protests Erupt in Kansas Against U.S. Immigration Enforcement
data.description: Hundreds of residents gathered in Lawrence, Kansas, on February 20 to protest recent actions by the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). According to Jin10, the demonstrators expressed their opposition to the enforcement activities carried out by immigration officers in the area and criticized the U.S. government's immigration policies. The protest reflects growing discontent among local communities regarding federal immigration enforcement measures.
